text: over to David. Thanks Ivan. Good afternoon and thank you for joining Sandisk's fiscal
speaker: OPERATOR

text: third quarter earnings call. We delivered another strong quarter with excellent
speaker: OPERATOR

text: performance across all key metrics reflecting the strength of the Sandisk
speaker: OPERATOR

text: franchise. Before turning to our end markets I'd like to provide an update on
speaker: OPERATOR

text: a priority we previously outlined. Last quarter we were engaged in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: discussions with customers on multi-year supply partnerships. What we refer to as
speaker: OPERATOR

text: new business models or NBM's. I am pleased to share that we have successfully
speaker: OPERATOR

text: advanced those conversations with five multi-year partnerships signed so far.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: These partnerships are structured to lock in committed supply for our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers and committed financials for Sandisk. Our customers commitments are
speaker: OPERATOR

text: backed by firm financial guarantees. These partnerships support durable
speaker: OPERATOR

text: structurally higher earnings and a significantly more predictable and less
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cyclical business for Sandisk. We believe this marks a fundamental evolution of our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: business which is centered on deeper customer alignment, enhanced visibility
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and long-term value creation. These MBM's reflect the strategic value of our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: world-class NAN technology which is built on decades of innovation. The
speaker: OPERATOR

text: investment we've made in R&D and manufacturing including tens of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: billions of dollars in cumulative CAPEX and IP have built the foundation for a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: powerful new business model in which we manage the full stack from front-end
speaker: OPERATOR

text: manufacturing through chip and system level design to final back-end
speaker: OPERATOR

text: assembly and test. Both the extension of our joint venture with Kyoksha and the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: supply agreement for DRAM following our investment in NANIA further strengthen
speaker: OPERATOR

text: our supply chain resiliency. This leverage is enabling us to drive stronger
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customer engagement allowing long-term conversations with partners who value
speaker: OPERATOR

text: technology performance and long-term supply assurance. With increased
speaker: OPERATOR

text: engagement in the optionality across the portfolio we can optimize our end
speaker: OPERATOR

text: market mix more effectively. Together these transformations have resulted in a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: step change in what we believe to be sustainable gross margins, free cash
speaker: OPERATOR

text: flow generation and earnings power in a market that we expect to grow in the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: double digits for the foreseeable future. Data Center is a clear example of this
speaker: OPERATOR

text: strategy in action with revenue growing 233% sequentially. This milestone
speaker: OPERATOR

text: reflects years of preparation and our deliberate shift toward what is now the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: most strategic and fastest growing end market. While we have made substantial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: progress there is significant growth opportunities ahead driven by the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: fundamental shift in underlying infrastructure requirements of artificial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: intelligence. We are witnessing extraordinary growth not just in model
speaker: OPERATOR

text: size but in resulting token generation the duration and complexity of model
speaker: OPERATOR

text: runs in the increasing importance of context. As AI model scale from billions
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to trillions of parameters in deployments advanced from simple inference to deep
speaker: OPERATOR

text: reasoning in increasingly autonomous agentics systems NAN has become a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: critical component of the underlying infrastructure. Infants optimizations
speaker: OPERATOR

text: such as KV cash along with workloads like rag require substantial high
speaker: OPERATOR

text: performance low latency flash to deliver real-time responsiveness and quality of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: user experience. These workloads expand the amount of data that now needs to be
speaker: OPERATOR

text: stored on low latency flash which is well beyond the model itself as systems
speaker: OPERATOR

text: must retain context intermediate data and large external data sets. As a result
speaker: OPERATOR

text: NAN flash is emerging as the only economically viable solutions deliver
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that capacity performance and efficiency required to keep models
speaker: OPERATOR

text: accessible for real-time inference at scale. This shift in understanding the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: critical nature of our technology comes at a time when our product
speaker: OPERATOR

text: differentiation is strongest anchored in what has been recognized as an
speaker: OPERATOR

text: industry gold standard for NAN technology with Bix8 and a broad leading
speaker: OPERATOR

text: portfolio with TLC and QLC offerings. We are confident that our world-class
speaker: OPERATOR

text: product portfolio and technology leadership will continue to drive data
speaker: OPERATOR

text: center customers to see sand disc as a partner of choice over the long term and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we are already seeing that preference translate into results. Our fiscal third
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quarter revenue was enhanced by strong demand for our TLC based enterprise
speaker: OPERATOR

text: SSD portfolio which powers performance intensive compute workloads where speed
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and latency are paramount. Looking ahead to the fiscal fourth quarter we expect
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to begin shipping our QLC Stargate solutions for revenue adding another
speaker: OPERATOR

text: layer of revenue growth. Together TLC and QLC serve distinct but complementary
speaker: OPERATOR

text: roles reflecting how we are deliberately architecting our portfolio to meet
speaker: OPERATOR

text: evolving customer needs with our broad portfolio of AI focused data center
speaker: OPERATOR

text: products. In Edge we are seeing a continued shift towards premium devices
speaker: OPERATOR

text: across both PC and smartphone markets. These platforms are increasingly
speaker: OPERATOR

text: incorporating on-device capabilities which are driving higher storage
speaker: OPERATOR

text: requirements and greater demand for high performance solution. As a result our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: mix continues to shift to high value configuration and customers that assign
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the appropriate value to our technology. Consumers saw strong year over year
speaker: OPERATOR

text: revenue growth across all key storage categories and regions despite evolving
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consumer industry dynamics. This performance was supported by our strong
speaker: OPERATOR

text: brand recognition and channel presence as we focused on the most financially
speaker: OPERATOR

text: attractive demand. In February we unveiled our next generation portable SSD
speaker: OPERATOR

text: portfolio designed to support faster more demanding workflows and AI enabled
speaker: OPERATOR

text: content creation. This launch reinforced our innovation and leadership in the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: SSD category generating meaningful external visibility with coverage across
speaker: OPERATOR

text: multiple global media outlets. We also continued to strengthen global consumer
speaker: OPERATOR

text: engagement through new brand led go-to-market activities such as our space to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: hold more campaign which is driving deeper customer connection by localizing
speaker: OPERATOR

text: global narratives and engaging diverse communities worldwide. Together these
speaker: OPERATOR

text: efforts reflect our focus on our end markets and commitment to driving demand
speaker: OPERATOR

text: through brand recognition product innovation and strong go-to-market
speaker: OPERATOR

text: execution as we shift our portfolio toward higher value opportunities and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: transition away from legacy upsell models. Our broad end market exposure sets us
speaker: OPERATOR

text: apart and we remain committed to serving customers across these markets. With that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'll turn the call over to Luis to dive deeper into our financial performance
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and guidance. Thank you David. I will begin with an update on our new business
speaker: OPERATOR

text: models or MBMs which are designed to provide us with demand certainty and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: provide our customers with supply assurance. We find three agreements in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the third quarter and an additional two so far in the fourth quarter and we're
speaker: OPERATOR

text: currently in active negotiations with several other customers. These agreements
speaker: OPERATOR

text: are tailored to meet the needs of our customers and in aggregate provide us
speaker: OPERATOR

text: with demand certainty and financials that we expect will be consistent with our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: fiscal fourth quarter guidance. The duration of this agreement varies with the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: longest contract extending to five years. In aggregate volume commitments
speaker: OPERATOR

text: increased during the life of the contracts with quarterly commitments
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and a combination of fixed and variable pricing. These agreements with variable
speaker: OPERATOR

text: pricing allow us to capture upside if prices rise while allowing our customers
speaker: OPERATOR

text: some upside if prices decline over time. As you will see in our thank you the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: three contracts signed during the quarter provide minimum contractual
speaker: OPERATOR

text: revenue of approximately 42 billion dollars. We will update you as we make
speaker: OPERATOR

text: more progress. Each contract is secured with financial guarantees that protect us
speaker: OPERATOR

text: if the purchase obligations are not fully performed by our customers. In
speaker: OPERATOR

text: aggregate the five agreements signed so far include financial guarantees that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: exceed 11 billion dollars and include prepayments and other financial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: instruments managed by third party financial institutions. Out of these
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agreements 0.4 billion dollars in prepayments are included in our Q3
speaker: OPERATOR

text: balance sheet. These five new business models account for over a third of our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: business model bids in fiscal year 2027 which we expect to increase as we
speaker: OPERATOR

text: conclude additional agreements over the next few months. We expect these new
speaker: OPERATOR

text: business models to reshape the historical cyclicality of our business
speaker: OPERATOR

text: improving visibility and results in pricing and margins that reflect the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: value of our technology and investments ultimately delivering higher more
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consistent and durable returns for shareholders. Moving on to our results
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we have a number of different options. We have a number of different options
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that we have in order revenue for the third quarter was 5,950 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars up 97% sequentially and up 251% year-over-year. These compares
speaker: OPERATOR

text: favorable to our guidance of 4,400 to 4,800 million dollars and was driven by
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the new business model. Our bids shipment were flat year-over-year and down
speaker: OPERATOR

text: high team sequentially as we build higher inventory levels primarily to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: support strong BEX-8 QLC demand in the fourth quarter, Stargate ramp and to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: prepare for our recently signed new business models. In line with our mid to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: high team growth model, bids shipments increased 18% fiscal year to date.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Now to the end markets sequentially data center revenue grew 233% to 1,467 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars. Edge grew 118% to 3,663 million dollars and consumer came in at 820 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars down 10% in line with our historical systemality. Our portfolio
speaker: OPERATOR

text: planning strategy focuses on delivering attractive long-term economics with the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: allocation remaining a core strength. We remain committed to serving all three
speaker: OPERATOR

text: end markets to maximize long-term value creation. Our non-GAP gross margin for the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: third quarter was 78.4% up from 51.1% in the prior quarter. This compares
speaker: OPERATOR

text: favorably to our guidance of 65 to 67% and was driven by our shift towards
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the value mix and the overall pricing environment. Non-GAP operating expenses
speaker: OPERATOR

text: for the third quarter were 448 million dollars and represent 7.5% of revenue as
speaker: OPERATOR

text: compared to 13.7% of revenue in the prior quarter as we generate additional
speaker: OPERATOR

text: leverage. This compares favorably to our guidance range of 450 to 470 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars. As a result, non-GAP operating margin were 70.9% off from 37.5% in the prior quarter.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Non-GAP PPS was 23.41, off from 6.20% in the prior quarter. This compares
speaker: OPERATOR

text: favorably to our guidance range of 12 to 14 dollars.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Key GAP to non-GAP reconciliation items include 20 million dollars in stock-based
speaker: OPERATOR

text: compensation, net of taxes, which represents 0.3% of revenue. And 46 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars related to the write-off of onamortized issuance fees as a result of our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: repayment of the remaining 650 million dollar balance in our TLB. We close the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quarter with 3,735 million dollars in cash and cash equivalents on our balance sheets.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Moving on to free cash flow. During the quarter we generated $2,955 million in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: adjusted free cash flow, which represents a 49.7% margin. Cash flow from operations
speaker: OPERATOR

text: came in at 3,038 million dollars partially offset by 83 million dollars from net
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cash capital spending. Gross capital expenditures totaled 240 million dollars and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: represented 4% of revenue. Our capital plan is designed to balance growth
speaker: OPERATOR

text: opportunities and generate attractive returns while supporting our ongoing
speaker: OPERATOR

text: big aid transition. We remain highly disciplined in how we evaluate such
speaker: OPERATOR

text: investments to protect long-term sustainability of our business financials.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Moving on to guidance. For the fourth quarter we forecast revenue between 7,750 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to 8,250 million dollars from both bids, growth and higher pricing. Our forecast for
speaker: OPERATOR

text: non-GAP gross margin is between 79 and 81%. We expect non-GAP operating expenses
speaker: OPERATOR

text: between 480 and 500 million dollars as we continue to invest in innovation on R&D.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We expect non-GAP interest and other income between 10 and 30 million dollars and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: non-GAP tax expenses between 775 and 875 million dollars. We forecast non-GAP
speaker: OPERATOR

text: EPS between 30 and 33 dollars assuming a 158 million fully diluted shares. Moving to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: capital allocation. The priorities we outlined in February of last year were to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: invest in the business, achieve a net cash position and then return cash to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: shareholders. In line with these priorities we have taken steps over the last two
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quarters to solidify our supply chain including extending our JV with Kyok
speaker: OPERATOR

text: shut through December 2034 and investing approximately 1 billion dollars in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: India to secure long-term DRAM supply. We have also taken actions that put us in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: a strong net cash position by paying off the remaining balance of our TOB. Given
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the strong progress today we are announcing that our board of directors have
speaker: OPERATOR

text: authorized a 6 billion dollar share by back program of outstanding shares of common
speaker: OPERATOR

text: stock. Their repurchase of authorization is affecting immediately with no expiration
speaker: OPERATOR

text: date. With that I'll turn the call back to David for closing remarks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you Luis. In summary we continue to execute with conviction at a critical
speaker: OPERATOR

text: inflection point for this business. NAND has always been a foundational
speaker: OPERATOR

text: technology. Empowering the world's best in class semi-conductor storage solutions
speaker: OPERATOR

text: required to drive the largest technological movements including PC, mobile, cloud
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and now artificial intelligence. Data center has become our fastest growing market
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and the workloads driving that demand including inference, reasoning and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agentic systems represent a structural and durable shift in how the world's most
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consequential technology is built and deployed. Our new business models reflect
speaker: OPERATOR

text: this shift. Five signed agreements to date over 11 billion dollars in financial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: guarantees and over a third of our bits in fiscal year 2027 under firm customer
speaker: OPERATOR

text: commitments represent a fundamental reshaping of our business providing
speaker: OPERATOR

text: visibility, pricing protection and more consistent durable returns. Our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: technology and product portfolio are intersecting this extraordinary demand
speaker: OPERATOR

text: at exactly the right moment equipped with a complete portfolio that now includes
speaker: OPERATOR

text: a scaled and rapidly growing enterprise SSD business. We are allocating
speaker: OPERATOR

text: supply to the highest value opportunities and establishing a new pillar of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: growth for sand disc. This progress is converged in a single moment. We believe
speaker: OPERATOR

text: our margins are sustainable. We've achieved our net cash target and we've
speaker: OPERATOR

text: announced plans to return capital to shareholders through buybacks all while
speaker: OPERATOR

text: reinforcing our operational foundation. Combined with our multi-year NBM's in the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: acceleration in the data center and market, this gives us both financial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: strength and structural resilience. The result is a durable growth model,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: a valuable franchise and a business built to generate substantial sustained
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cash flow. With that Ivan, let's see if there's any questions.

text: So it's great to be speaking with you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, Dave, I wanted to talk about the, you know, you said one third of bit growth
speaker: OPERATOR

text: next year is contracted.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Where do you see this going to based on your conversation?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Should we expect that every year, you know, you're getting, you know, it's one third next
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year, but like every year, can this be above 50% where you know how much is kind of done
speaker: OPERATOR

text: going into the year?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then I have a follow up.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So first of all, welcome.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're glad you're here.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's good to hear your voice again.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So look, I would say a couple of things to this.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: One is we're just, we're still in a lot of conversations about how we're changing this
speaker: OPERATOR

text: business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And you know, it takes a little bit of while, it takes a while depending on the customer.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Some customers are, you know, come into the conversation really concerned about multi-year
speaker: OPERATOR

text: supply agreements.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And so it's an easier conversation.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Other customers come into the conversation very used to the way the market has worked
speaker: OPERATOR

text: in the past where, you know, they commit volume and want to negotiate price every quarter.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That's not the kind of agreement we're interested in.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're interested in agreements that give a certainty of economics and, you know, a key
speaker: OPERATOR

text: point of what we said in the script, I want to make sure it's understood.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, these, there are fixed and variable elements of these agreements, but we're targeting
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the five agreements we've signed.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're targeting financials that are in line with what we just forecasted to.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So this is very, very, very attractive business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Now then we're still in active conversations for our supply going forward.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That includes next year, all the way through the next five years.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So I expect the number that that's, you know, we said at least a third.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we're over a third.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I expect that number to go up over the next, next several quarters.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Where can it get to?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I definitely think it can get above 50 percent.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And, you know, but we'll see.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think we can drive it actually quite, quite high.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we have a desire to drive it, drive it quite high.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Great.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then just with regard to, you know, margins, Dave, I mean, do you feel like with when you
speaker: OPERATOR

text: do these kind of agreements that you can lock in margins?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, your stocks not trading as if you're going to stay at 80 percent anyway.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But when you do these kinds of things, is there a target margin and will you be willing
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to kind of, you know, share what range at least it's in?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, you could argue the stocks trading like your margins are going to go back into
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the 40s or something or, you know, something like that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So do you have a target margin that is in the range that you know, in a range that you're
speaker: OPERATOR

text: comfortable talking about?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, I don't think we're there yet to talk about that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, I mean, we're, you know, when we get a little further along in this, we'll wrap
speaker: OPERATOR

text: this all up in a new model for everybody.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But, you know, we're, we're very proud of our technology.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Let's put it that way.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think we're finally for the first time in decades in this business, getting to the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: point where the value of our technology is getting recognized, at least for us.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, quite frankly, the value of our technology has been recognized in the market.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's just other people have been collecting that value and it hasn't been the producers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think that now we're getting a more even distribution of those of that value.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, you know, we're not necessarily interested in trading away that value for certainty.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're interested in getting that value and getting certainty as well.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And to your point, like we understand how the franchise is valued.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we're very, very focused on getting the cyclicality out of this business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's corrosive.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's corrosive to the way we invest our cap backs.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's corrosive to our customer's ability to get a sufficient amount of product to drive
speaker: OPERATOR

text: their spectacular businesses.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think if we've taken some very meaningful steps down this path now, we've got very significant
speaker: OPERATOR

text: commitments from very significant customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think as we can continue to proceed down this path, we will move this entire business
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to a very different spot to everybody's benefit.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our benefit, our investors benefit, our customers benefit, and their customers benefit because
speaker: OPERATOR

text: they're building just spectacular technology.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we're a key part of that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, I think we're on that journey.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think it was, you know, very questionable if we could even make that progress.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I've had a lot of people tell me in the last year, it's never going to happen.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's happening.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But we're still in the early stages.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And as we make continued progress, we'll continue to give everybody updates on that progress.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you very much.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks, Ben.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from CJ Muse with Cancer Fitzgerald.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Good afternoon.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks for taking the question.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I guess curious to get your thoughts around supply demand going forward for NAND.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's fairly interesting in the sense that we're getting only limited greenfield, mostly
speaker: OPERATOR

text: layer count driving bit growth, where new greenfield is really being prioritized for
speaker: OPERATOR

text: DRAM.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, within that kind of construct as well as, you know, the Agentic AI kind of incremental
speaker: OPERATOR

text: growth, how are you thinking about, you know, when the industry might get into balance?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, I think CJ, you know, my point of view on this is the industry is always in balance,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: right?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Markets always balance supply and demand.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think the question is, you know, implicit in your question of what I hear is if you
speaker: OPERATOR

text: lower the price, will you meet more demand?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I mean, that's just, we're kind of working around that whole environment.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Look, let me start on, make a couple of points.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: On the demand side, we continue to see data center accelerate.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, before what we saw this week, you know, we would raise even our calendar year
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 26 data center growth number to the mid-70s from where we were in the 60s, just three
speaker: OPERATOR

text: months ago, which is up from the 40, three months before that and the 20s, three months
speaker: OPERATOR

text: before that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, we continue to see very, very strong growth in the data center.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Outside of data center, we're seeing some contraction in the market just because of unit
speaker: OPERATOR

text: decline that's to be expected, although we expect to see that bounce back in 27.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Now on the supply side, I think this is a major, major benefit of this franchise is
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that we can increase supply through nodal transitions.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Right?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We have a very, very productive R&D pipeline.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: This is something we've invested in for a very, very long time with our JV partner
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and the Bix Roadmap.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And so we can continue to drive, you know, the bit growth we're talking about mid to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: high teens through nodal transitions.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We don't need to, you know, we need to add some clean room space because each node has
speaker: OPERATOR

text: more steps and more steps is more tools.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So there is some additional CAPEX.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But it's not like other markets that you reference where you actually have to add capacity because
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you're not getting that much from the nodal transition.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, you know, quite frankly, I think this is what makes this franchise such a spectacular
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cash generator is because the amount of CAPEX we need to invest, especially CAPEX as a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: percent of revenue is continuing to go down substantially.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The absolute CAPEX is still there.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm just saying that relative to our revenue generation, and, you know, we've made all
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the investments in the nodal transition.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we have years of runway into what our nodes are going to be and what the bit growth is
speaker: OPERATOR

text: going to be from those.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we will continue to invest in those and drive those nodal transitions to grow the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: market in that mid to high teens' rates.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And that's basically what we see across the NAND players, quite frankly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Very helpful.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I guess just real quickly in terms of capital structure, you're now no debt, 3.7 billion
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cash.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, what do you think you need to retain given your view today and given kind of the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: new contracts that you're signing?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then how should we think about kind of buybacks from here?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we did announce a share-by-back program with this call, right?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We just announced a $6 billion buy-back.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we'll keep on tracking our cash flow.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're generating good cash.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And as things change, and as we execute the share-by-back program, we'll keep you updated,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: CJ.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from Jim Schneider with Goldman Sachs.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Good afternoon.
speaker: OPERATOR

speaker: OPERATOR

speaker: OPERATOR

text: at the beginning, we have some very meaningful customers who are joining and some more that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we're working with, but we can't disclose the name of our customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think to your second part of your question, Jim, we will provide you with your questions.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You, this RPO metric, which I think is very interesting, which is how much of the business
speaker: OPERATOR

text: is already contracted.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And that's based on minimal prices.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we will continue to give that information every quarter, and you have that visibility

text: because we make progress quarter-over-quarter.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then maybe as a quick follow-up, could you maybe talk about, you know, giving these
speaker: OPERATOR

text: new business models and your visibility on customer demand, what is the state of your

text: discussions with Kyokseah in terms of potentially increasing bit supply and, you know, are you
speaker: OPERATOR

text: contemplating anything above the sort of 20% range of growth that you've outlined previously?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: No, we still have the same plans and the conversations with Kyoksear are always very robust and very
speaker: OPERATOR

text: ongoing.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, the teams are working on this every single day, but we have our Bix-A transition
speaker: OPERATOR

text: plan that we've aligned on, and we're executing to it, and it's going extremely well.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks, Jim.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Visit Jim.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from Aaron Rakers with Wells Fargo.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And this is Jake on for Aaron.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Congrats on the great results, guys.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, just to start off looking at Stargate starting to shift for revenue and for Q, can
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you just give maybe some color on how meaningful that RAM could be over the next few quarters?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, look, we have a whole other, there's two major products in the data center space.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think we've talked about this a lot.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: There's the compute focus, what we consider kind of compute focused enterprise SSD lower
speaker: OPERATOR

text: capacities, much higher speeds, and then there are interface speeds, and then there's much,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: much higher densities.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Stargate is going, and, you know, the progress we've seen so far in the portfolio is coming
speaker: OPERATOR

text: off of that compute focused TLC drive, and now we're going to bring the whole QLC product
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to market, which has been under qualification with some major players for, you know, well
speaker: OPERATOR

text: over a year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, you know, we're not going to forecast a specific market segment, but, you know,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we're very proud of that product, and we think it's going to do quite well in the market.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Okay, thanks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then, you know, maybe as a follow on with some more powerful LMs released over the past
speaker: OPERATOR

text: few weeks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I guess how are you thinking about the KB cache opportunity as we see a Gen.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Take AI grow?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Has that meaningfully changed over the last few quarters, and maybe how customer discussions
speaker: OPERATOR

text: have changed there?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, you know, I think that we've advanced our understanding of that a lot over the last
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quarter to since it became a major part of the conversation.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I even think the team did a webinar on that, which we'd be happy to repeat if folks are
speaker: OPERATOR

text: interested.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think when you really start to drill into that opportunity, and you try and size it,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you know, it obviously gets very complicated very quickly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, what are the number of concurrent sessions that are going to be run?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: What's the average input tokens?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: What's, you know, cache hit ratios?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Storage, durations?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, so there's a lot of elements to that, and I think what it says is kind of where
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you are going, which is we need to stay very close to our customers because they're
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the ones that are going to have all the detail on the infrastructure they're building, the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: ones that are doing infrastructure at scale are going to have the great insight into how
speaker: OPERATOR

text: are all those variables put together against the use cases they believe they're building
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And you know, I think this just reinforces this, you know, this business model question.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Because our customers go through those calculations and understand the significance of NAN that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that could drive.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That is a good foundation for the conversation about striking deals, you know, two years,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: three years, five years in length that are very, very substantial in the amount of, amount
speaker: OPERATOR

text: of demand.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, we're talking about five deals and more than a third of our portfolios.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, you know, it's an extremely, extremely dynamic situation.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think these are all the things that go into kind of understanding where this market is
speaker: OPERATOR

text: right now and how fast it's moving.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's literally moving every single day.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And you know, even out for those of us on the inside of the market that see the data points
speaker: OPERATOR

text: literally hour over hour, it is moving very, very rapidly as people start to, you know,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: our customers really start to understand the dynamics of the infrastructure they're
speaker: OPERATOR

text: building.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think, you know, it feels very good about, you've been able to stay very close
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to them.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, they're obviously very close to our technology and our products.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: They're responding very positively to those products.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: They understand that they're, you know, they're willing to commit years of purchasing with,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you know, financial, a financial model around that that is very attractive for us.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And it gives them a very attractive attribute, which is guaranteed supply.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then quite frankly, they're willing to put a very large financial commitment up that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: basically guarantees that ongoing demand.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think that's a very big part of what we're talking about.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, I think that we've talked about these agreements a lot.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We've gotten a lot of feedback from a whole lot of people.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: There's a lot of, there's a lot of sometimes talk in the market that they won't hold.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: They won't have teeth, all these kinds of things.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I can tell you nothing can be further from the truth.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We have customers that are literally putting up billions of dollars of collateral and through
speaker: OPERATOR

text: various financial instruments that will survive for the life of these contracts.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And if they don't meet their obligations on consistent purchasing every quarter, then
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that financial commitment immediately comes to us as a compensation for that contract
speaker: OPERATOR

text: not being concluded.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So I actually don't, you know, probably never expect to collect those because I think our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers are extremely serious about needing this product.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I can tell you the normal cases, we sign an agreement.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And within weeks, we're having a conversation about how we increase the amount of product
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we can get to them over that timeframe.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So a very dynamic market, things are changing very quickly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It does make it difficult to forecast in the things you see.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, you see the results were able to put up and, you know, they're significantly better
speaker: OPERATOR

text: than we thought they were three months before.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And that's because the market's just moving very, very quickly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And, you know, the pieces change, you know, literally day by day.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And, you know, what we're doing is we want to solve, you know, a whole bunch of issues
speaker: OPERATOR

text: for our business in this.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We know we have great technology.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We've made enormously substantial investments in intellectual property.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, we've been building the Bix Roadmap for decades.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We have enormous investments in fabs, some of the largest fab complexes in the world
speaker: OPERATOR

text: with our JV partner.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And you know, we want to leverage all of that, get a fair return for our products, and get
speaker: OPERATOR

text: this cyclicality out of the business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Because like I said, I think it's quite frankly, it's corrosive for everybody that's in this
speaker: OPERATOR

text: industry.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And, well, I shouldn't say it.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I can't speak for everybody.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But I think what's happening is there are now customer sets that, you know, very substantial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers that don't want to play the quarter by quarter price game.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: They have spectacular businesses, and they understand that we provide a very important
speaker: OPERATOR

text: component to their spectacular business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And they want to make sure that they have the best products, which we believe we provide.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We know we provide those, and they want them on a very consistent basis so they can continue
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to plan their own business.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That is opening this opportunity.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think the fundamentally change the way this business has worked over the last several
speaker: OPERATOR

text: decades.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And quite frankly, that's a lot of fun for us to do that because, you know, customers
speaker: OPERATOR

text: are very happy with those agreements.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: They're very happy with them.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And like I said, I think everybody wins.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm trying to figure out who doesn't win in this equation.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And so far, every agreement we've signed, the customers have been thrilled to get to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the point of actually getting it signed.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So anyway, probably maybe to take a little longer answer than you were looking for.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But we feel very, very good about where we're at.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we think that we're now starting down a path that is, you know, as Ben said, quite
speaker: OPERATOR

text: frankly, there's lots of other technology industries that understand how to do this.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: This is not reinventing the wheel.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're just using techniques that people associate with, you know, other businesses that are
speaker: OPERATOR

text: recurring revenue models, but certainly everybody that's well, not everybody.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If you run a whole bunch of technology business, you understand how recurring revenue works.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And it's a very, very powerful financial model.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we think we can bring it to our franchise.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Congrats again.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.

text: in both now, as you would expect.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we see those flattening out up slightly in 27 is kind of our internal view of the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: world.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And quite frankly, I think that that's just a reflection of market's ability to adapt,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: right?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, I think that's, if you're in business, and I think especially the device business,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: those are spectacular companies, very, very smart people that run them.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And they understand how to change their portfolio mix and, you know, what they need to do
speaker: OPERATOR

text: given the environment they're in to drive their spectacular business forward.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think there's no doubt there's an adjustment process right now, and it's happening.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think we'll get through that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And I think, you know, we'll get back to a point where we're still going to see content
speaker: OPERATOR

text: per device increase this year, at least on phones, PCs, we've got it flat.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we'll see both of those start to inflect up next year while units are flat to up slightly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So now what will we supply?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So that's an interesting question.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we're going to supply the customers that we have agreements with.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That's the way we're starting to look at the market.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think this is the change that we've been talking about.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're not going to wait until next year and see what the market gives us.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're talking to edge customers as well about these new business models, agreements,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and their multi-year agreements with all the characteristics we've talked about in the past,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: committed growing demand, same kind of structure we talked about for these first five.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So, you know, those customers understand their businesses extremely well.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're engaged in those kind of conversations.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We'll see if we reach the finish line on some of those.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm sure we will.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And that's how we'll have great insight into what their demand is,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: because they will have told us and they will have put a financial commitment behind it,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: which will allow us to plan a lot better what demand we're going to serve.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we're kind of navigating out of this market where we just show up and kind of see what demand is and see what the price is
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and then adjust our mix very rapidly.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We know how to do that, but where we'd rather go is the path we're down,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: which is customers commit to us what their demand is and they committed in a way that we can really count on it.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And quite frankly, they can really count on us.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you, your last question on CapEx.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, David just talked about our philosophy.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We continue to invest towards, you know, kind of a meetings, CapEx capacity growth over time.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: What this translates to into dollars, so we usually think about it as percent of revenue, but more in dollars,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you know, is a little bit of an increase into the next several quarters.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: As we did the easier conversions first and there, the next conversions will be a little bit more expensive on a dollar basis
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to deliver that same kind of growth.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Nothing dramatic, but just as you are modeling things, I would put in a few, a little bit more,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: higher CapEx per as with transition, but not a change in our philosophy, as David mentioned earlier.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from Vijay Rakesh with Mizuho.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, hi. Thanks, David and Luis, a phenomenal set of results here.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Just a quick question on the RPO and the financial guarantees that I see your data center is already 1.5 billion,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: so that's like an annualized 6 billion run rate on the data center side.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If all the 11 billion and the 42 billion RPO mostly all in data center, is that a fair assumption?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And to the pricing question, on these guarantees, it's probably marked to market as you look out two or three years.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: There has to be some benchmarking to the market.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Is that fair?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Follow?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, so we're not disclosing the customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So I leave it at that, but if you look at the 42 billion, not a lot of companies would be able to do that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So that's our RPO and that's the minimum contractual revenue that you would expect from the three deals that we signed
speaker: OPERATOR

text: before the end of the quarter.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So if you include the other five, that would be a larger number and you will see that number in our next quarter,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: but it's not part of the 42.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If you think about the 11 billion, which I think is the second part of your question,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: there are different financial instruments that we're using to protect those.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: There is a portion that is in prepayments.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: As I mentioned in my prepared remarks, you will see that in the thank you, that somewhere around $400 million that you will see in our balance sheet.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And there are other financial instruments and that's probably as far as I can go, which are managed by third-party financial institutions
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that are triggered if there is a breach in the contract that doesn't go all the way to the end.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So that's kind of how it works, Vijay.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: On the, as you look at your NAN SSD roadmap, you have a pretty disruptive technology coming down the pipe in terms of high bandwidth flash.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Any thoughts on how that's progressing, all that's going, if you can give us some colors?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, we're happy with how it's going.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, it's a kind of steady as she goes.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're having conversations with customers on how they would deploy it.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're building the technology, the NAN die itself, the controller.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So we're still on the timeline.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We talked about earlier of having actual NAN, the NAN late this year and look for more of a system with the controller early mid next year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from Blaine Curtis with Jeffries. Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hey, guys.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Good afternoon and great results. Yeah, maybe just following on that, I had a question about you're hearing a lot more discussion about different memories, hearings, maybe accelerators using more DRAM.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm just kind of curious if you had any perspective.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: There's a previous question on KB cache.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think everybody just assumed there'd be a lot more hard SSDs to serve back.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm just kind of curious as you look at that future roadmap and different memory tiering.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I've been with flash fits into that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm just kind of curious if there's any change of a lot.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Quarter. So I'm just the thoughts of where that sort will be.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I don't know. I mean, I think the kind of tiering architecture that's, you know, came out maybe a quarter ago is what's being deployed.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I've been with flashes. Again, it's not a necessary substitute for an enterprise SSD or something like that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's a way to bring a lot more density to inference in a little different way. We, you know, we'd have to follow up on that with a little more detail.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But look, we're seeing, you know, you can see it in our numbers. We're just seeing an enormous amount of a pull on that portfolio of high performance enterprise SSDs as these architectures get deployed.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Deployed and inference starts to get deployed at scale.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: As we said in the prepared remarks, I mean, NAND is just a big part of that architecture now given the size of models, the size of KV cash.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The context length, all these things is, you know, your NAND is the most scalable semiconductor technology in the world.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It's now front and center. Well, it's now a critical component of that architecture.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we're seeing that be pulled through. And, you know, I still expect this will be refined as we go forward. Again, this is why, again, why we're staying very close to our customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our customers are the ones that know the big customers that are deploying this at scale.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, there, I suspect they have, well, I know they have very, very detailed insight into how they're going to scale this across a global franchise.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And so, you know, understanding what that is and what that means for demand on our product.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think that there's an enormous amount of work going on there. And that, again, that's what's driving the demand signals that are years into the future for us.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And allowing us to align our business model around that demand.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from, excuse me, Amit Daryani with Evercore ISI. Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Good afternoon. This is Victor Santiago on for Amit. Thanks for squeezing me in.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I want to ask about the non-investment and supply agreement last month.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Could you help us better understand the strategic rationale for it?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Is that primarily the security ramp or access to HVF or choosing memory products going forward?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah. As you have seen, you know, our data center business is doing pretty well, and we just posted very interesting growth.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And that's just with our TOC product and a lot more to come as we continue to expand our business now with QLC.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And as we drive growth through our new business models.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And one of the key things that we need to have access is DRAM.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The partnership with NANIA provides an investment into the company and gives us a preferential treatment of access to DRAM as well.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So that's the rationale.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, thank you. And as a follow-up, I believe you provided the duration on the longest contract that you signed.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Give us an idea on what the average duration might be across the five deals you signed so far.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, frankly, we cannot get into that level of detail.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We're giving, I think you know, the minimums we signed before were much shorter, but we're not giving an average.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I apologize for that, Victor.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I think Victor, one thing, we want a portfolio of deals like, you know, one year, two year, three year, five year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So that they don't all end at the same time and, you know, all those kinds of things and we don't face clips.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I mean, we have every expectation will renew some of these.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You know, some of the deals are for a certain number of years with options for more years.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So it's still a little bit early, but you know, we're going about this where it's not just, there's not a fixed template.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It depends on the customer.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Every deal is kind of customized to specific customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And you know, as we get a little deeper into this, maybe we can have some of the conversations and some of the numbers you're asking for.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But right now it's a little bit early for that.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next question comes from Wamsi Mohan with Bank of America.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hi, thanks for taking my question. It's Rupulu filling in for Wamsi.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I just had two quick ones, one for Luis.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: On the long term agreements, is there any restriction on when you can raise prices?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Is it allowing for annual price increases or are there certain conditions when you can raise prices?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And then one for Dave, how do you see the interest in QLC flash trending and how do you see the mix of TLC versus QLC trending over the next couple of quarters?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, so sorry, I'm going to frustrate you a little bit.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We can't go into pricing in detail for each of the contracts, as I said.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: At the beginning, there are some fixed price components and some variable pricing components.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And it is very different depending on each of the agreements that we have.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So there is no kind of an overall answer, overall on pricing.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: So TLC, QLC, I mean, if you look across whole portfolio, roughly two thirds TLC, one third TLC.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If you look at data center, obviously for us, it's predominantly TLC and we'll be launching major TLC products next quarter, but there's a lot of demand for TLC,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and these perform, especially in the enterprise SSD space.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Given the inference architectures and some of the comments earlier on KV cache and how important it is and quite frankly how it can scale dramatically based on your assumptions of the use case you're serving,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: there's a very, very strong demand on TLC.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But you know, that said, we expect our QLC products to do very well.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: This concludes our question and answer session.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I would like to turn the conference back over to Ivan Donaldson for any closing remarks.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, I just want to say thank you everyone for joining the call today.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you for your support and we look forward to speaking with you throughout the quarter.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The conference has now concluded.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you for attending today's presentation.
